w:r.w:t: This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) defines the complete functional and non-functional requirements for the Opportunity Intelligence Platform — a curated, timeline-driven opportunity discovery and tracking system for undergraduate students. The document is intended for use by the development team, project stakeholders, and evaluators at VJStartups.

The system, internally codenamed Campus Opportunity Aggregator (COA), addresses a critical gap in how undergraduate students discover time-sensitive opportunities such as internships, research positions, fellowships, competitions, hackathons, scholarships, mentorships, workshops, and incubators.

The platform is explicitly not a job board. Its core differentiator is timeline visibility — surfacing what is coming, when to prepare, and what is at risk of being missed — replacing accidental senior-knowledge-transfer with a structured, always-current intelligence system.
Single institution deployment (one college, one branch enum set).
Three user types: Student, Admin, Organisation (Source).
MERN stack: MongoDB, Express.js, React (React Router v6), Node.js.
No scraper integrations (architecture is expandable — targeted at V2).
No OAuth in V1 (email/password authentication only).
No public submission form — Organisations access a dedicated webpage and Listing Manager.
Campus Opportunity Aggregator — the system being specified.
A single opportunity record (internship, competition, etc.) in the system.
An external entity (club, department, company) that submits listings. Called 'Source' in schema; displayed as 'Organisation' in UI.
A categorised slug (e.g., 'frontend', 'machine-learning') used to classify listings and match student interests.
A record linking a student to a listing with a tracked status (saved / applied / ignored / missed).
Boolean flag on a Listing indicating admin verification. Curated listings display a verified badge.
A scheduled background task run by the server at defined intervals.
JSON Web Token — used for session authentication.
Document XML Attribute — unit used in DOCX layout (1440 DXA = 1 inch).
Time-To-Live — MongoDB index that auto-expires documents after a set duration.
Admin-set number of weeks before a deadline to trigger a preparation reminder notification.
Derived field on a Listing timeline. Computed at read time from deadline date vs lastDeadline age.

Section 1 — Introduction. Section 2 — Overall System Description. Section 3 — Functional Requirements (grouped by module). Section 4 — Non-Functional Requirements. Section 5 — External Interface Requirements. Section 6 — System Constraints and Design Decisions.

The Opportunity Intelligence Platform is a standalone, web-based SaaS application deployed on free-tier cloud infrastructure (Vercel for frontend, Render for backend, MongoDB Atlas for database). It has no integration with existing college ERP or placement portals in V1. The system is self-contained and communicates with external services only via email (Nodemailer/SMTP).
The system consists of three logical tiers:
Presentation tier — React SPA served via Vercel CDN.
Application tier — Node.js/Express REST API deployed on Render.
Data tier — MongoDB Atlas (M0 free cluster) with Redis for feed caching (pre-computed Recommended section).
Enrolled undergraduate; study year 1–6; single institution.
Browse feed, save/track listings, view calendar, manage notifications.
Faculty or designated staff. Manages the platform's content.
Curate listings, manage sources, author guides, review submissions, manage tags and users.
/admin/* routes. JWT-gated with role: admin check.
Club, department, or company submitting opportunities. Has its own account.
Submit new listings via Organisation Listing Manager. View own submissions.
Dedicated /org/* webpage. JWT-gated with role: source. No admin panel access.
Internal cron jobs and event-driven services.
Auto-transition listing states, fire notifications, flag stale records, mark missed activities.
Internal only. No external API exposure.
Client: Modern web browsers (Chrome 100+, Firefox 100+, Safari 15+). Responsive design targeting desktop and mobile viewports.
Server: Node.js 18+ LTS. Hosted on Render (free tier, spin-up latency acceptable for V1).
Database: MongoDB Atlas M0 (512 MB). Connection via Mongoose ODM.
Cache: Redis (Upstash free tier) for pre-computed Recommended feed sections.
Email: Nodemailer with SMTP relay (Gmail App Password or SendGrid free tier).
CDN / Hosting: Vercel for React SPA (automatic CI/CD from main branch).
All students belong to a single institution. Branch names are enumerated (not free text).
Study year is self-reported by the student at onboarding (1–6). No batch-year arithmetic.
Organisations are pre-registered by Admin. No self-registration in V1.
Email delivery is non-transactional — occasional delays acceptable (daily digest model).
Admin is trusted — all listing edits are accepted without re-verification workflow.
Scrapers are not integrated in V1. The schema supports them but no scraper code is written.
roadmap.sh DomainTag links are display-only; no live API calls to roadmap.sh in V1.
The following design decisions were made prior to specification and are treated as constraints:
User inputs currentYear directly. No batchYear arithmetic.
Listing archived on deadline pass. Admin runs cycle reset manually.
No auto-reset. Dead listings stay out of feed.
Soft-delete (isActive: false). Orphaned refs silently ignored.
Existing listings remain visible. isActive: false blocks new submissions only.
'unknown' is a valid enum value rendering as 'Not specified'.
Simpler schema. No confidence scores per field.
UI label only. Drives no cron logic.
Only curated listings get prep reminders.
Admin must manually set to real status. Does not auto-graduate.
Unknown = not yet reviewed by admin.
Not built in V1. sourceType enum and hooks exist.
V2 additive — no breaking changes needed.
Organisations get a dedicated authenticated webpage + Listing Manager.
Single institution → branch is an enum, not free text.
Requirements are grouped by module. Each requirement is identified by a code in the format FR-[MODULE]-[NN]. Priority is designated P1 (must-have), P2 (should-have), or P3 (nice-to-have).

The system shall accept name, email, and password (minimum 8 characters). Backend shall hash the password with bcrypt (cost factor 12) and create a User record with isEmailVerified: false and onboardingComplete: false.
On signup, the system shall send an email containing a time-limited JWT verification link (24-hour expiry). GET /api/auth/verify?token=... shall set isEmailVerified: true and redirect to /onboarding.
The system shall return HTTP 409 if the email already exists in the database.
POST /api/auth/login shall validate credentials via bcrypt.compare. A successful login returns a JWT (7-day expiry) and updates lastLoginAt. An unverified email returns HTTP 403 with a clear message.
The frontend shall store the JWT in localStorage and include it in the Authorization header for all authenticated API calls.
The system shall present a multi-step onboarding flow. Step 1 collects the student's academic branch (enum from the institution's branch list) and current study year (selector, values 1–6).
Step 2 shall display all active DomainTags grouped by category. The student shall select up to 5 tags. Tags are loaded from GET /api/tags?active=true.
Step 3 shall display notification preference toggles (all enabled by default). The student may adjust before completing.
PUT /api/user/onboarding shall save all onboarding fields and set onboardingComplete: true. The student is redirected to /app/feed.
All /app/* routes shall redirect to /onboarding if onboardingComplete: false. All /app/* and /admin/* routes shall redirect to /login if the JWT is absent or invalid.
Users with status: suspended shall be blocked from accessing /app/* routes, receiving HTTP 403.
On login, if role === 'admin', the frontend shall redirect to /admin. If role === 'source', redirect to /org.
GET /api/feed/sections shall return all four sections in a single API call: Closing Soon, Recommended for You, Don't Miss, and Browse All. Sections are computed via four parallel DB queries.
Closing Soon shall return up to 5 open listings whose deadline falls within 7 days from the current date, excluding listings the user has ignored, sorted by deadline ascending.
Don't Miss shall return up to 3 listings with priority: dont-miss and status: open, sorted by deadline ascending.

The recommendation engine shall first filter listings to those where the student's currentYear is in targetAudience.years (or the array is empty — meaning all years eligible) AND the student's branch is in targetAudience.branches (or empty array). Listings already saved, applied, or ignored shall be excluded.

Each eligible candidate shall receive a score: +2 per matching domainTag with user interests, +1 if deadline is within 30 days, +2 if priority is high, +3 if priority is dont-miss. Results sorted by score descending; top 8 returned. If fewer than 3 results, the year filter is relaxed and the query reruns.

The Recommended section shall be pre-computed by a background job running every 15–30 minutes and cached in Redis keyed by userId. The feed endpoint shall serve from cache on load, not re-compute on demand.

GET /api/feed/browse shall accept query parameters: domain[] (multi-select), type[] (multi-select), stipend, location, page, and limit (default 20). Results shall be filtered to status in [open, upcoming]. Sort: priority descending, deadline ascending.
Browse All filter state shall be persisted in URL query parameters (React Router). Page refresh shall restore filters.
The Save button on a listing card shall POST /api/activity with { listingId, status: 'saved' }. The UI shall update optimistically and roll back with a toast error if the API call fails.

The Ignore button shall POST /api/activity with { listingId, status: 'ignored' }. The card shall disappear from feed immediately (optimistic). Ignored listings shall not reappear in Closing Soon or Recommended.
The feed page shall display a tracking summary (Saved / Applied / Missed counts) sourced from GET /api/activity/summary.
The top navigation shall display an unread notification count badge sourced from GET /api/notifications/unread-count.
On feed page load, the three API calls (feed/sections, activity/summary, notifications/unread-count) shall fire in parallel.
GET /api/listings/:id shall return the full listing document with computed confidenceLevel and dataSourceYear attached. The source's name and verificationLevel shall be populated.
Closed or cancelled listings shall not be returned to students unless they have a saved or applied UserActivity record for that listing.
The listing detail page shall render: organisation logo (or initials fallback), title, orgName, type badge, stipendType, and locationType.
A status pill shall display the current listing state (Open, Closing Soon, Upcoming, Closed, Cancelled). A stale warning shall be shown if isStale: true.
If isCurated: true, a 'Verified listing' indicator shall be displayed.
The action bar shall reflect the current UserActivity.status for the authenticated user. Buttons: Save, Mark Applied, Ignore. The notes text field shall be unlocked when status is 'applied'.

The timeline card shall display the deadline with a human-readable confidence label: 'Confirmed' (deadline set for this cycle), 'Based on [YEAR] data' (lastDeadline within 1 year), or 'Approximate — [YEAR] data' (older). Expected window shown when no confirmed deadline exists. Prep start suggestion shown if prepLeadWeeks is set.
The eligibility block shall list applicable branches and years. It shall display 'You appear eligible' or 'Check eligibility' based on whether the student's currentYear and branch match.
If guideId is set on the listing, a 'Prep guide available' link shall navigate to /app/guide/:id.
The source organisation's name and verificationLevel badge shall be displayed.

A flag button shall open a modal with an issueType selector (dead_link, wrong_deadline, wrong_eligibility, duplicate, other) and an optional proposedFix text field. Submission creates a DataFlag record via POST /api/listings/:id/flag.
An external link button shall navigate to the listing's externalUrl in a new browser tab.
GET /api/activity?status=all shall return all UserActivity records for the authenticated user with listing data populated (title, deadline, orgName, status).
Three metric cards (Saved, Applied, Missed) shall display counts from GET /api/activity/summary. Tapping a card shall filter the activity list.
Students shall be able to transition a saved listing to applied via PUT /api/activity/:listingId with { status: 'applied' }. This shall set statusUpdatedAt and write an AuditLog entry.
Students shall be able to remove any non-missed activity record (deletes the UserActivity document). Missed items are not removable.
Missed items shall be displayed last in the activity list, visually de-emphasised. The count is intentional — it builds student awareness of missed opportunities.
The status 'missed' shall only be set by the system cron job. The student API PUT endpoint shall reject requests to set status: 'missed' with HTTP 403.
All status transitions shall be reflected optimistically in the UI. On API failure, the UI shall roll back with a toast notification.

GET /api/notifications?page=1&limit=20 shall return notifications sorted by createdAt descending (most recent first). Both read and unread are included. Expired notifications are auto-removed by TTL index.
Unread notifications shall have a distinct background colour. Each row shows: type icon, payload.title, payload.message, relative timestamp.

Tapping a notification shall call PUT /api/notifications/:id/read (setting status: 'read') and navigate to payload.actionUrl. The userId check on the backend shall prevent reading another user's notification.
A 'Mark all read' button shall call PUT /api/notifications/read-all, updating all unread notifications for the user.
The system shall support five notification types: deadline_3day, deadline_1day, dont_miss, season_open, cancelled.
The notification payload (title, message, actionUrl) is a snapshot captured at creation time. It shall not change if the linked listing is later edited.
A MongoDB TTL index on the expiresAt field shall automatically delete expired notifications (deadline notifications expire at the listing's deadline date).
Don't-miss notifications are capped at 1 per user per week across all listings. Season-open notifications are capped at 1 per domain category per user per month.

GET /api/listings/calendar shall return all listings with status in [upcoming, open] that have at least one timeline date field (expectedStart, openDate, or deadline) populated. Response is a lightweight projection: listingId, title, orgName, timeline, status, priority, domainTags.
The calendar shall display 12 month columns (or stacked rows on mobile). The current month shall be highlighted.
Each month shall display listing chips for opportunities opening or closing in that month. Colour coding: green = opening this month, amber = closing this month, grey = upcoming.
Tapping a chip shall navigate to /app/listing/:id.
A toggle shall allow students to filter the calendar to show only listings matching their saved domain interests.
The settings page shall allow students to edit name, academic branch, and current year of study.
Students shall be able to update their domain interests using the same tag picker as onboarding (max 5 selections).
All five notification type toggles shall be adjustable. emailEnabled shall control whether email is sent in addition to in-app notifications.
PUT /api/user/profile shall persist changes. On success, the frontend shall invalidate the React Query feed cache so the next feed load uses the updated profile.
Students shall be able to change their password. The backend shall require the current password before accepting a new one.
GET /api/guides/:id shall return the guide document only if isPublished: true. A 404 shall be returned for unpublished or non-existent guides.
Guide content (stored as Markdown) shall be rendered using a Markdown renderer component in the student UI.
If the guide has linkedListings, a 'Related opportunities' section shall display links back to those listing detail pages.
Organisations shall access the system via a dedicated /org webpage. No public submission form exists in V1.
Organisation access is gated by JWT (role: source). Organisations are pre-registered by Admin.
Organisations shall have access to a Listing Manager that allows them to create and manage their own submitted listings.
Listings submitted by an Organisation (verificationLevel: unverified or verified) shall enter the Admin submission queue with status: unknown. They shall not auto-publish.
Listings submitted by a source with verificationLevel: official shall be directly published (configurable per-source by Admin).
GET /api/admin/listings shall support filtering by status, priority, isCurated, isStale. Results shall include aggregated engagement counts (saves, applies, ignores) via a UserActivity lookup.
The listing manager shall paginate results (default limit: 30 per page).
The admin nav shall display pending submission count and pending flag count as badges.
The 'New Listing' button shall navigate to /admin/listings/new, an empty curation panel.
Listings with isStale: true or lastVerifiedAt older than 270 days shall be visually indicated (red cell) in the table.
Bulk actions shall include: mark stale, bulk archive.
The curation panel shall expose all listing fields: content, audience, tags, timeline, status, priority, curation flags, and guide link.
confidenceLevel and dataSourceYear shall be displayed as derived read-only fields, computed live from the current form values.
PUT /api/admin/listings/:id shall reject saves if the submitted version field does not match the current DB version (HTTP 409). The admin must reload before re-saving.
Each successful save shall increment the listing's version field.
The right panel shall display the last 10 audit entries for the listing.

A 'Cycle Reset' button shall be available only when status: closed and scheduleType is recurring-annual or recurring-irregular. POST /api/admin/listings/:id/cycle-reset shall: copy deadline to lastDeadline, clear openDate and deadline, set status: upcoming, set isCurated: false, increment version. A confirmation dialog shall precede the action.
If admin sets status: cancelled, the API shall immediately trigger notification dispatch (type: cancelled) to all users with saved or applied activity on that listing.
If admin sets priority: dont-miss, the API shall immediately dispatch dont_miss notifications to users whose interests overlap the listing's domainTags, subject to frequency cap.
GET /api/admin/listings?status=unknown&sort=createdAt shall populate the queue with unreviewed submissions.
Approve navigates to the full curation panel pre-filled with submitted data. Admin fills missing fields, sets a real status, and saves.
Reject sets the listing status to 'closed' and records a rejection reason in AuditLog. The listing is removed from the queue but retained for history.
The source manager shall display a table of all organisations with: name, sourceType, verificationLevel, listing count, isActive toggle.
Verify cycles: unverified → verified → official. PUT /api/admin/sources/:id/verify sets verificationLevel, verifiedAt, and verifiedBy (acting admin's userId).
Deactivate requires a confirmation modal explaining that existing listings remain visible. PUT /api/admin/sources/:id/deactivate sets isActive: false. No cascade to listings.
Deactivation shall not affect existing listings from that source.
Display all tags with: slug, displayName, category, active listing count, active user interest count, isActive toggle.
Inline form: slug (auto-generated from displayName, editable), displayName, category. POST /api/admin/tags.

The retire flow shall display a modal showing how many listings and users reference the tag. Setting isActive: false soft-deletes the tag. If a replacement slug is provided, the system shall run a background migration updating all Listing.domainTags and User.interests.

Orphaned tag slugs (from retired tags) in User.interests and Listing.domainTags shall be silently ignored in queries (filter to active tags only). No migration is required when no replacement is specified.
GET /api/admin/guides shall return all guides (published and draft) with title, isPublished toggle, and linkedListings count.
The guide editor shall present a split-pane view: left side editable Markdown, right side live preview.
Admin shall be able to search listings by title and add them to the guide's linkedListings array.
Admin shall be able to use the tag picker to add domain tag slugs to linkedTags for general-purpose guides.
Publish sets isPublished: true. For each listingId in linkedListings, the system shall set Listing.guideId = guide._id if not already set.
Saving without publishing shall not make the guide visible to students.
GET /api/admin/listings?isStale=true&sort=lastVerifiedAt shall populate the staleness review queue.
PUT /api/admin/listings/:id/verify sets lastVerifiedAt: now and isStale: false without changing any other fields. AuditLog entry written.
Each row shall link to the full curation panel for comprehensive review.
The audit log viewer is entirely read-only. No edit or delete functionality shall be exposed.
Filter bar shall support: category, actorType, dateRange, referenceId (paste a listing/user/source ID to see its full history).
The diff column shall render an expandable JSON cell showing field-level before/after values for edit actions and from/to for status transitions.
AuditLog records are immutable. The backend shall never expose an update or delete endpoint for AuditLog.
Searchable table: name, email, role, status, createdAt, lastLoginAt.
Admin can change a user's role (student / admin / source) via a dropdown with confirmation dialog. The change requires an AuditLog entry.
Admin can toggle user status between active and suspended. Suspended users are immediately blocked from /app/* routes on next request.

Runs every hour. (1) Opens listings where status: upcoming AND openDate ≤ now. (2) Closes listings where status: open AND deadline ≤ now. Copies deadline → lastDeadline on close. (3) Sets UserActivity records to missed where status: saved AND listing deadline ≤ now. statusUpdatedAt is set to the listing's actual deadline, not the cron run time.

Runs daily at 08:00. Sends 3-day deadline reminders (in-app + email if enabled) and 1-day deadline reminders (in-app only) to users with saved activity. Checks notificationPrefs.deadlineReminders. Prevents duplicate notifications per (userId, listingId) pair.

Runs every Sunday at 02:00. Sets isStale: true on listings where lastVerifiedAt < 270 days ago (or is null) and status is in [upcoming, open]. Also flags listings where lastDeadline is older than 2 years.

Runs 1st of every month at 09:00. Finds listings where timeline.expectedStart = currentMonth and status: upcoming. Dispatches season_open notifications to matching users (interests overlap domainTags, seasonAlerts enabled, not already notified this year). Cap: 1 per domain category per user per month.

Event-driven (not cron). Triggered immediately when admin sets priority: dont-miss. Dispatches dont_miss notifications to users with matching interests, subject to frequency cap (max 1 per user per 7 days across all listings).
Event-driven. Triggered immediately when admin sets status: cancelled. Creates cancelled notifications for all users with saved or applied activity. In-app + email if emailEnabled.
Background job running every 15–30 minutes. Recomputes Recommended listing sets for all active users using the eligibility + scoring logic. Stores top 8 results per user in Redis keyed by userId.

The Main Feed page shall render four sections within 2 seconds on a standard broadband connection, achieved by serving the Recommended section from Redis cache and running Closing Soon / Don't Miss queries in parallel.
All non-feed authenticated API endpoints shall respond in under 500 ms (P95) under normal load.
The pre-computed Recommended section shall be refreshed at least every 30 minutes. Cache misses shall trigger on-demand computation and cache population.
All query patterns described in Section 3 shall be covered by a DB index as documented in the HLD. Full-collection scans shall not occur in production query paths.
All passwords shall be stored as bcrypt hashes with cost factor 12. Plaintext passwords shall never be logged or returned in API responses.
All /app/* and /admin/* API routes shall validate the JWT on every request. Expired or tampered tokens shall return HTTP 401.
Admin-only endpoints shall verify role: admin server-side. Organisation-only endpoints shall verify role: source. Client-side role checks are supplementary only.
Notification and activity endpoints shall enforce userId scoping (users cannot read or modify another user's data).
The system shall apply rate limiting. Organisation submission endpoints are rate-limited as appropriate.
All API inputs shall be validated server-side using a schema validation library (e.g., Zod or Joi). Invalid inputs shall return HTTP 422 with field-level error messages.
JWT tokens, passwords, and contact emails shall not appear in server logs.
The system architecture accepts the cold-start latency of Render's free tier (up to 30 seconds). A loading state shall be shown to users during cold start.
All student-facing mutations (save, ignore, mark applied) shall use optimistic UI with rollback on failure, ensuring a responsive experience despite network latency.
Cron jobs shall be idempotent — re-running a job shall not create duplicate notifications or double-transition statuses.
All MongoDB queries are designed around existing indexes. New query patterns shall require an index to be added before the route is deployed.
The schema and API are designed so all documented V2 features (scrapers, OAuth, Organisation accounts, full-text search) can be added without breaking changes to V1 data or API contracts.
Every state-changing operation (listing save, status change, user role change, tag retirement) shall write an AuditLog entry with a diff object.
No update or delete operations shall be permitted on AuditLog records.
The version field on Listing shall be used for optimistic concurrency checks in all admin save operations.
Retired DomainTags (isActive: false) shall be silently ignored in all queries. No migration is required unless a replacement slug is specified.
The application shall be a single-page application (SPA) built with React and React Router v6.
The design system follows the AccentSketch theme: a professional SaaS UI with a removable hand-drawn personality layer.
The colour palette is centred on dark navy (#1B2A4A) as the primary brand colour and amber (#E6A817) as the accent.
All student-facing pages shall be responsive and usable on mobile viewports (minimum 360px width).
Markdown content (listing descriptions, guide content) shall be rendered using a client-side Markdown renderer (e.g., react-markdown).
All API endpoints shall use REST conventions with JSON request/response bodies.
Authentication is performed via Bearer JWT in the Authorization header.
API versioning is not required in V1; all routes are under /api/*.
Error responses shall use standard HTTP status codes with a JSON body: { error: 'message', field?: 'field_name' }.
The API base URL for V1 is the Render deployment URL configured via an environment variable in the React app.
The application communicates with MongoDB Atlas via Mongoose ODM.
The connection string is stored as an environment variable (MONGODB_URI).
The Redis cache for recommendation pre-computation is accessed via the ioredis or upstash/redis client.
Outbound email is sent via Nodemailer using SMTP. The sending email address and SMTP credentials are stored as environment variables.
Email templates are plain HTML with inline CSS for maximum email client compatibility.
No inbound email processing is required in V1.
V1 serves only one college. Branch is an enum; institution-specific branch list is hardcoded.
MongoDB Atlas M0 (512 MB), Render free dyno, Vercel hobby plan. Cold-start latency accepted.
No automated scraping in V1. All listings are entered manually or via Organisation submissions.
Email/password authentication only. No Google or social OAuth in V1.
No unauthenticated listing submission. Organisations must be pre-registered by Admin.
Listing status transitions for submitted listings are Admin-controlled. No auto-graduation from 'unknown'.
scheduleType is a UI metadata field only. Cron logic is driven by timeline date fields, not scheduleType.
Null prepLeadWeeks means no prep reminder fires. No system default.
Admin listing saves use version-based optimistic concurrency. Conflicting saves are rejected with HTTP 409.
isUrlBroken is always false in V1. No background link-checking is implemented.
confidenceLevel and dataSourceYear are derived fields. They are never stored; always computed at read time.
End of Software Requirements Specification — COA-SRS-001 v1.0
